sourceMovie.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery for the same reason it broke through in 2022: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Rian Johnson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Knives Out
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery clicked for you, Knives Out (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery?
faq.answer: Try Knives Out, The Thursday Murder Club, Fletch—each shares DNA with Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery?
faq.answer: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery (2022) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Knives Out Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
